name: Sweet and Sour Peppers with Almonds (Mandorlata Di Peperoni)
recipeIngredient: 3 medium Red bell peppers (about 1-1/4 lbs.), Quartered and deribbed
2 medium Green bell peppers (about 3/4 lb.), Quartered and deribbed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, soak the raisins in warm water for 20 minutes; drain and set aside.
2. If desired, peel the peppers by cutting the quartered peppers in half lengthwise and flattening slightly. Use a swivel-bladed vegetable peeler to remove the skin, then cut into 1/2-inch strips. Alternatively, slice unpeeled peppers into 1/4-inch strips.
3. In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over moderate heat. Add the almonds and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2 minutes. Add the raisins and cook for 1 minute, then stir in the peppers, sugar, vinegar, and salt. Cover and cook for 20 minutes.
4. Uncover and continue cooking until the peppers are very tender and slightly glazed, about 10 to 15 minutes longer. If the peppers stick to the pan, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of warm water. Serve at room temperature.
This dish can also be prepared several hours ahead and reheated just before serving. It is delicious with grilled meat, especially Italian sausages. Peeling the peppers without roasting first makes them particularly succulent, but peeling is not mandatory.
